name: leidenalg (Leiden Community Detection Algorithm)
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL-3.0); Vincent Traag / Leiden University

performance.notes: C++ igraph backend; fast for million-node graphs

agent_readiness.notes: Leiden algorithm for community detection on igraph graphs. import leidenalg. import igraph as ig. G = ig.Graph.Famous('Petersen'). partition = leidenalg.find_partition(G, leidenalg.ModularityVertexPartition). partition.membership. partition.quality(). Resolution: leidenalg.RBConfigurationVertexPartition(G, resolution_parameter=1.0). Weighted: partition_type=leidenalg.RBConfigurationVertexPartition, weights='weight'. Scanpy integration: sc.tl.leiden(adata) uses leidenalg internally. Supersedes Louvain algorithm in quality.

gotchas: Stochastic — results vary between runs (seed parameter needed for reproducibility)
Requires igraph graph objects — convert from NetworkX if needed
Resolution parameter significantly affects partition granularity
best_when: Community detection on large graphs (better quality than Louvain)
scRNA-seq clustering via Scanpy (sc.tl.leiden uses this)
avoid_when: Very small graphs where results differ from Louvain minimally
summary: leidenalg implements the Leiden community detection algorithm. leidenalg.find_partition(G, ModularityVertexPartition). RBConfigurationVertexPartition for resolution parameter. Weighted graphs. GPL-3.0 by Vincent Traag/Leiden. Supersedes Louvain in community quality and well-connectedness. Used internally by Scanpy's sc.tl.leiden(). Requires igraph objects. Stochastic — set seed for reproducibility.
